title: Fix unused import warnings in secure_enclave contract
body:
## Issue Description
The `secure_enclave` contract has unused import warnings for `String` in both `lib.rs` and `test.rs`.

## Files Affected
- `contracts/secure_enclave/src/lib.rs:5`
- `contracts/secure_enclave/src/test.rs:4`

## Expected Behavior
No unused import warnings during compilation.

## Current Behavior
Compilation generates warnings:
```
warning: unused import: `String`
 --> contracts/secure_enclave/src/lib.rs:5:5
```

## Suggested Fix
Remove the unused `String` import from both files.

title: Add comprehensive unit tests for identity_registry contract
body:
## Issue Description
The `identity_registry` contract currently has 0 tests. We need comprehensive unit test coverage.

## Test Cases Needed
- Identity registration
- Identity verification
- Identity revocation
- Multi-signature support
- Edge cases and error conditions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Minimum 80% code coverage
- [ ] All public functions tested
- [ ] Error scenarios covered
- [ ] Integration tests with dependent contracts

title: Implement input validation for credential_registry
body:
## Issue Description
The `credential_registry` contract lacks proper input validation for credential metadata.

## Security Concern
Without proper validation, malformed credentials could be stored, potentially leading to:
- Storage bloat
- Invalid credential states
- Potential attack vectors

## Required Validations
- [ ] Credential ID format validation
- [ ] Expiry date must be in the future
- [ ] Metadata size limits
- [ ] Required fields validation
- [ ] Signature verification

title: Optimize storage usage in zk_verifier contract
body:
## Issue Description
The `zk_verifier` contract uses persistent storage inefficiently, leading to higher transaction costs.

## Current Issues
- Storing redundant verification keys
- Not cleaning up expired proofs
- Inefficient data structures

## Optimization Suggestions
1. Use temporary storage where appropriate
2. Implement proof expiration and cleanup
3. Compress verification keys
4. Batch verification support

## Expected Impact
- 30-40% reduction in storage costs
- Faster verification times

title: Add event emissions to drug_discovery contract
body:
## Issue Description
The `drug_discovery` contract doesn't emit events for critical operations, making it difficult to track on-chain activity.

## Missing Events
- Research milestone completions
- Trial phase transitions
- Result publications
- Collaboration additions/removals

## Event Schema Example
```rust
#[contracttype]
pub enum DrugDiscoveryEvent {
    MilestoneReached { research_id: BytesN<32>, milestone: u32 },
    TrialPhaseUpdated { research_id: BytesN<32>, phase: u32 },
    ResultPublished { research_id: BytesN<32>, timestamp: u64 },
}
```

title: Fix error handling in cross_chain_bridge
body:
## Issue Description
The `cross_chain_bridge` contract doesn't properly handle cross-chain transaction failures.

## Current Behavior
When a cross-chain transaction fails:
- No rollback mechanism
- Funds could be locked
- No error notification to users

## Required Fixes
- Implement atomic swap pattern
- Add timeout and refund mechanism
- Emit failure events
- Add retry logic with exponential backoff

title: Add rate limiting to provider_directory searches
body:
## Issue Description
The `provider_directory` contract's search function can be called unlimited times, potentially enabling DoS attacks.

## Proposed Solution
Implement rate limiting:
- Max 10 searches per address per hour
- Configurable by admin
- Exempt verified healthcare institutions

## Implementation Notes
- Use sliding window algorithm
- Store rate limit data efficiently
- Allow admin to adjust limits

title: Implement missing access controls in anomaly_detector
body:
## Issue Description
Several admin functions in `anomaly_detector` lack proper access control checks.

## Affected Functions
- `update_threshold`
- `add_validator`
- `pause/unpause`
- `clear_alerts`

## Security Risk
Any address can call these functions, potentially:
- Manipulating detection thresholds
- Adding malicious validators
- Disabling the system

## Fix Required
Add `require_admin()` checks to all admin-only functions.

title: Add integration tests for payment_router with escrow contracts
body:
## Issue Description
No integration tests exist for the interaction between `payment_router` and escrow contracts.

## Test Scenarios Needed
- Payment flow with successful escrow release
- Payment refund on dispute
- Multi-party payment splitting
- Timeout handling
- Fee calculation accuracy

## Expected Coverage
- Happy path scenarios
- Error handling
- Edge cases (minimum/maximum amounts)
- Gas optimization verification

title: Refactor large lib.rs files exceeding 2000 lines
body:
## Issue Description
Several contracts have monolithic `lib.rs` files that are difficult to maintain:

## Affected Contracts
- [ ] ai_analytics (2500+ lines)
- [ ] predictive_analytics (2200+ lines)
- [ ] healthcare_oracle_network (2100+ lines)
- [ ] telemedicine (excluded, but needs fixing)

## Refactoring Strategy
1. Extract type definitions to `types.rs`
2. Move helper functions to `utils.rs`
3. Separate business logic into modules
4. Keep only contract interface in `lib.rs`

## Benefits
- Improved readability
- Easier testing
- Better code organization
- Faster compilation

title: Add missing documentation for public API functions
Implement circuit breaker pattern for healthcare_payment
body:
## Issue Description
The `healthcare_payment` contract lacks emergency stop functionality for critical failures.

## Circuit Breaker Requirements
- Emergency pause by authorized addresses
- Automatic trigger on anomaly detection
- Gradual resume capability
- Event emissions for state changes

## Implementation
```rust
pub enum CircuitState {
    Closed,    // Normal operation
    Open,      // Emergency stop
    HalfOpen,  // Testing recovery
}
```

## Triggers
- Manual admin trigger
- Anomaly detection integration
- Threshold-based automatic triggers

title: Fix Soroban SDK version inconsistencies across contracts
body:
## Issue Description
Some contracts use hardcoded SDK versions instead of workspace dependencies.

## Affected Contracts
- patient_gamification: hardcoded 21.7.7
- credential_notifications: hardcoded 22.0.10

## Impact
- Dependency conflicts
- Build failures
- Inconsistent behavior

## Fix
Update all contracts to use:
```toml
[dependencies]
soroban-sdk = { workspace = true }
```

## Verification
Run `cargo build` after changes to ensure compatibility.

title: Add fuzzing tests for critical contract functions
body:
## Issue Description
Critical contract functions lack fuzzing tests to discover edge cases.

## Priority Functions for Fuzzing
- Payment processing functions
- Access control checks
- Data validation logic
- Cross-chain message handling

## Tools
- Use `cargo-fuzz` for Rust fuzzing
- Integrate with CI pipeline
- Target 100+ hours of fuzzing per function

## Expected Outcomes
- Discover hidden bugs
- Validate input handling
- Improve robustness
- Security assurance

title: Implement proper decimal handling for financial calculations
body:
## Issue Description
Financial calculations in payment contracts use integer arithmetic, leading to precision loss.

## Affected Contracts
- healthcare_payment
- payment_router
- escrow
- token_sale

## Issues
- Rounding errors in fee calculations
- Loss of precision in splits
- Inaccurate interest calculations

## Solution
Implement fixed-point decimal library:
- Use 18 decimal places (standard in DeFi)
- Proper rounding modes
- Overflow protection
- Comprehensive test coverage

## Reference
Consider using `soroban-sdk` decimal types or implement custom FixedPoint128.

title: Add benchmarking suite for contract performance
body:
## Issue Description
No performance benchmarks exist to track contract execution costs.

## Benchmark Requirements
- Gas usage per function
- Storage read/write costs
- Execution time measurements
- Memory usage profiling

## Implementation
```rust
#[test]
fn benchmark_provider_search() {
    let start = env.ledger().timestamp();
    // Execute function
    let end = env.ledger().timestamp();
    assert!(end - start < MAX_EXECUTION_TIME);
}
```

## CI Integration
- Run benchmarks on each PR
- Track performance regression
- Alert on significant degradation

title: Implement upgrade testing framework
body:
## Issue Description
No systematic testing for contract upgrades and migrations.

## Test Requirements
- State preservation during upgrades
- Backward compatibility
- Migration script validation
- Rollback capability

## Framework Components
1. Deploy contract v1
2. Add test data
3. Upgrade to v2
4. Verify state integrity
5. Test new functionality
6. Verify old functionality still works

## Automation
- Integrate with CI/CD
- Automated rollback on failure
- Version compatibility matrix

title: Add gas optimization for batch operations
body:

Batch operations in multiple contracts are not gas-optimized.

- Batch credential verification

## Current Issues

- Unnecessary iterations
- Inefficient data structures

## Expected Savings
- 40-60% gas reduction for batch ops
- Better user experience
- Lower transaction costs

title: Implement comprehensive logging for audit trail
body:

Insufficient logging for healthcare compliance requirements (HIPAA, GDPR).

## Required Logs
- Data access events
- Permission changes
- Record modifications
- Authentication attempts
- Cross-chain transfers

```rust
pub struct AuditLog {
    pub timestamp: u64,
    pub actor: Address,
    pub action: ActionType,
    pub target: BytesN<32>,
    pub result: OperationResult,
    pub metadata: Map<Symbol, String>,
}
```

## Compliance
- Immutable log storage
- Retention period enforcement
- Access control for logs
- Export capability

title: Add missing error codes for edge cases
body:
## Issue Description
Contracts return generic errors instead of specific error codes for debugging.

## Missing Error Codes
- InsufficientFunds
- DeadlineExceeded
- InvalidSignature
- UnauthorizedCaller
- ContractPaused
- StorageFull
- CrossChainTimeout

## Implementation
Update error enums in each contract:
```rust
#[contracterror]
#[repr(u32)]
pub enum Error {
    InsufficientFunds = 10,
    DeadlineExceeded = 11,
    // ... etc
}
```

## Benefits
- Better debugging
- Improved user feedback
- Easier monitoring

title: Implement automated security scanning in CI
body:
## Issue Description
No automated security scanning in the CI pipeline.

## Required Scanners
- [ ] cargo-audit for dependency vulnerabilities
- [ ] cargo-geiger for unsafe code detection
- [ ] Custom linting rules for security patterns
- [ ] Secret detection in commits

## CI Integration
```yaml
- name: Security Audit
  run: |
    cargo audit
    cargo geiger
    ./scripts/security-scan.sh
```

## Alerts
- Block PR on critical vulnerabilities
- Warning on medium issues
- Weekly security report

title: Add contract size monitoring and limits
body:
## Issue Description
No monitoring of contract WASM sizes, risking deployment failures.

## Stellar Limits
- Max contract size: 64KB
- Max transaction size: 64KB

## Monitoring Requirements
- Track WASM size per contract
- Alert at 80% threshold
- Optimization recommendations
- Size trend tracking

## Implementation
Add to CI:
```bash
for contract in contracts/*/; do
  size=$(wc -c < ${contract}*.wasm)
  if [ $size -gt 51200 ]; then
    echo "Warning: $contract exceeds 50KB"
  fi
done
```

title: Implement proper random number generation for gamification
body:
## Issue Description
Patient gamification contract uses predictable random sources.

## Security Risk
Predictable randomness enables:
- Reward manipulation
- Gaming the system
- Unfair advantages

## Solution
Implement VRF (Verifiable Random Function):
- Use oracle-based randomness
- Commit-reveal scheme
- Block hash with future block commitment
- Chainlink VRF integration (if available)

## Implementation Notes
Never use:
- `env.ledger().sequence()` alone
- Timestamps as sole entropy
- User-controllable inputs

title: Add contract interaction diagrams to documentation
body:
## Issue Description
No visual documentation showing how contracts interact with each other.

## Required Diagrams
1. System architecture overview
2. Payment flow diagram
3. Identity verification flow
4. Cross-chain interaction flow
5. Data access pattern diagram

## Tools
- Use Mermaid.js for markdown diagrams
- Update docs/ directory
- Include in README.md

## Example Format
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Patient] -->|Consent| B[Consent Contract]
    B -->|Access Grant| C[Medical Records]
    C -->|Log Access| D[Audit Contract]
```

title: Implement deprecated function warnings for upgrades
body:
## Issue Description
No mechanism to warn about deprecated functions during contract upgrades.

## Implementation
Add deprecation attributes:
```rust
#[deprecated(since = "v2.0.0", note = "Use new_function instead")]
pub fn old_function(env: Env) {
    // Emit deprecation event
    env.events().publish(
        (Symbol::new(&env, "Deprecated"),),
        "This function will be removed in v3.0.0"
    );
}
```

## Requirements
- Track deprecated functions
- Emit events on usage
- Documentation updates
- Migration guides

```rust
    // Iterate and remove expired items

}
```

## Incentives

title: Fix inconsistent naming conventions across contracts

title: Implement multi-signature support for admin operations
body:
## Issue Description
Admin operations rely on single signature, creating centralization risk.

## Required Features
- Multi-sig threshold configuration
- Proposal creation and voting
- Time-locked execution
- Emergency override mechanism

## Implementation
```rust
pub struct MultiSigConfig {
    pub signers: Vec<Address>,
    pub threshold: u32,
    pub timelock_duration: u64,
}
```

## Use Cases
- Contract upgrades
- Parameter changes
- Emergency pauses
- Fund withdrawals

## Security Benefits
- Reduced single point of failure
- Distributed trust
- Transparent decision making

title: Add performance profiling tools for contract development
body:

No tooling to profile contract performance during development.

```bash
make profile CONTRACT=medical_records
```

```
Execution Time: 45ms
```

title: Implement proper secrets management for test configurations
body:
## Issue Description
Test configurations may contain hardcoded secrets or test keys.

## Security Risks
- Exposed private keys in tests
- Hardcoded admin addresses
- Test network credentials in code

## Solution
- Use environment variables
- Implement .env.example template
- Add .gitignore for .env files
- Use CI secrets for test credentials

## Implementation
```rust
#[test]
fn test_with_config() {
    let admin_key = std::env::var("TEST_ADMIN_KEY")
        .expect("TEST_ADMIN_KEY must be set");
    // Use in test
}
```

## Audit
- Scan codebase for hardcoded secrets
- Remove any found
- Document secure testing practices

title: Add contract deployment verification scripts
body:
## Issue Description
No automated verification after contract deployment.

## Verification Steps
1. Contract deployed successfully
2. Initialization completed
3. Basic functionality test
4. Event emission check
5. Storage state validation

## Script Example
```bash
#!/bin/bash
verify_deployment() {
    local contract=$1
    local network=$2
    
    # Verify deployment
    soroban contract invoke \
        --id $contract \
        --network $network \
        --function test_ping
    
    # Check events
    # Validate state
}
```

## Integration
- Post-deployment hook
- CI/CD pipeline
- Rollback on failure

title: Implement comprehensive input sanitization
body:
## Issue Description
Insufficient input sanitization in data handling contracts.

## Security Concerns
- String injection attacks
- Oversized input storage abuse
- Invalid character sequences
- Malformed data structures

## Sanitization Rules
- Max length enforcement
- Character whitelist validation
- Format validation (emails, IDs, etc.)
- Null byte removal
- Unicode normalization

## Implementation
```rust
fn sanitize_input(input: String) -> Result<String, Error> {
    if input.len() > MAX_LENGTH {
        return Err(Error::InputTooLong);
    }
    // Additional validation
}
```

## Testing
- Fuzz testing for edge cases
- Invalid input rejection
- Boundary condition testing

title: Add dependency vulnerability monitoring
body:
## Issue Description
No automated monitoring for dependency vulnerabilities.

## Required Setup
- Dependabot configuration
- cargo-audit in CI
- Weekly vulnerability scans
- Alert notifications

## Implementation
Create `.github/dependabot.yml`:
```yaml
version: 2
updates:
  - package-ecosystem: "cargo"
    directory: "/"
    schedule:
      interval: "weekly"
    open-pull-requests-limit: 10
```

## Response Plan
- Critical: Fix within 24 hours
- High: Fix within 1 week
- Medium: Fix within 1 month
- Low: Track and schedule

title: Add comprehensive health check endpoints
body:
## Issue Description
No health check mechanism to monitor contract status.

## Health Check Functions
```rust
pub fn health_check(env: Env) -> ContractHealth {
    ContractHealth {
        version: VERSION,
        is_paused: Self::is_paused(&env),
        storage_usage: Self::storage_usage(&env),
        last_activity: Self::last_activity(&env),
    }
}
```

## Monitoring Metrics
- Contract version
- Pause status
- Storage utilization
- Recent activity
- Error rates
- Gas usage trends

## Integration
- Monitoring dashboard
- Alert thresholds
- Automated reporting

title: Fix potential integer overflow in token calculations
body:
## Issue Description
Token-related calculations may overflow without proper checks.

## Affected Areas
- Token sale calculations
- Reward distributions
- Fee computations
- Staking rewards

## Current Risk
Using standard arithmetic without overflow checks can lead to:
- Incorrect token amounts
- Fund loss
- Exploitation opportunities

## Fix Required
Use checked arithmetic:
```rust
let total = amount.checked_add(fee)
    .ok_or(Error::Overflow)?;
```

## Testing
- Test with maximum values
- Test boundary conditions
- Fuzz testing for edge cases
- Verify overflow detection

title: Implement comprehensive API versioning strategy
body:
## Issue Description
No versioning strategy for contract APIs, making upgrades difficult.

## Versioning Scheme
- Semantic versioning for contracts
- API version tracking
- Backward compatibility guarantees
- Deprecation timeline

## Implementation
```rust
pub const API_VERSION: u32 = 2;

pub fn get_api_version(env: Env) -> u32 {
    API_VERSION
}
```

## Migration Path
- Version negotiation
- Compatibility layer
- Sunset timeline for old versions
- Documentation updates

## Benefits
- Safe upgrades
- Client compatibility
- Clear deprecation path

title: Add missing clippy configuration for healthcare contracts

title: Add missing Soroban SDK feature flags utilization
body:
## Issue Description
Contracts don't utilize available Soroban SDK feature flags for optimization.

## Available Features
- `testutils` for testing
- `alloc` for dynamic allocation
- Custom feature flags

In Cargo.toml:
```toml
[dependencies.soroban-sdk]
version = "21.7.7"
features = ["testutils"]
```

- Update all contracts

title: Implement proper data retention policies
body:

No data retention policies implemented, violating healthcare regulations.

## Regulatory Requirements
- HIPAA: 6 years minimum
- GDPR: Right to be forgotten
- Local healthcare laws

## Implementation
```rust
pub struct RetentionPolicy {
    pub data_type: DataType,
    pub retention_period: u64, // seconds
    pub auto_delete: bool,
}

pub fn enforce_retention(env: Env) -> Result<u32, Error> {
    // Delete expired data
}
```

## Policy Types
- Medical records: Permanent (hashed)
- Audit logs: 6 years
- Temporary data: 90 days
- User preferences: Until deletion request

## Compliance
- Automated enforcement
- Deletion requests handling
- Audit trail for deletions

title: Fix inconsistent error message formatting
body:
## Issue Description
Error messages are inconsistent across contracts, making debugging difficult.

## Current Issues
- Mix of descriptive and generic errors
- No error code standardization
- Missing context in errors
- Inconsistent error categories

## Standardization
```rust
#[contracterror]
#[repr(u32)]
pub enum Error {
    // Access Control (100-199)
    Unauthorized = 100,
    InsufficientPermissions = 101,
    
    // Validation (200-299)
    InvalidInput = 200,
    InputTooLong = 201,
    
    // State (300-399)
    ContractPaused = 300,
    NotInitialized = 301,
}
```

## Documentation
- Error code registry
- Meaning and resolution
- Examples for each error

title: Implement proper contract initialization patterns
Add missing Soroban event indexing for queries
body:
## Issue Description
Events are not structured for efficient querying and indexing.

## Current Issues
- Inconsistent event topics
- Missing indexed fields
- Poor event structure for filtering

## Best Practices
```rust
env.events().publish(
    ("transfer", sender_address.clone(), receiver_address.clone()),
    TransferData { amount, timestamp }
);
```

## Event Standards
- Topic 1: Event type
- Topic 2: Primary address
- Topic 3: Secondary address (if applicable)
- Data: Detailed information

## Benefits
- Efficient filtering
- Better analytics
- Easier debugging
- Standard tooling compatibility

title: Add missing Soroban diagnostic events for debugging
body:
## Issue Description
Insufficient diagnostic events for troubleshooting contract issues.

## Diagnostic Events Needed
- Function entry/exit
- State changes
- Validation failures
- Authorization checks
- Error conditions

## Implementation
```rust
env.diagnostic().debug().record(
    &env,
    "Function called: update_profile",
    ("provider", &provider)
);
```

## Event Categories
- INFO: Normal operations
- WARN: Unusual but acceptable
- ERROR: Failed operations
- DEBUG: Development only

## Configuration
- Enable/disable per environment
- Production: ERROR only
- Staging: WARN+
- Development: All levels

title: Implement proper access control inheritance pattern
body:
## Issue Description
Access control logic duplicated across contracts instead of using inheritance.

## Proposed Pattern
Create base contract:
```rust
pub trait AccessControl {
    fn require_admin(env: &Env) -> Result<(), Error>;
    fn require_role(env: &Env, role: Role) -> Result<(), Error>;
    fn has_permission(env: &Env, address: &Address, permission: Permission) -> bool;
}
```

## Benefits
- DRY principle
- Consistent access control
- Easier auditing
- Simplified updates

## Implementation
- Create access_control module
- Migrate existing contracts
- Update tests
- Document patterns

title: Add comprehensive load testing framework
body:
## Issue Description
No load testing to validate contract performance under stress.

## Test Scenarios
- Concurrent transactions
- High-frequency calls
- Large dataset operations
- Peak load simulation

## Framework Setup
```rust
#[test]
fn load_test_provider_search() {
    let num_requests = 1000;
    let concurrent = 50;
    
    // Execute concurrent requests
    // Measure response times
    // Validate success rate
}
```

## Metrics
- Transactions per second
- Average response time
- P95/P99 latency
- Error rate under load
- Resource utilization

## CI Integration
- Nightly load tests
- Performance regression detection
- Capacity planning data

title: Fix Soroban SDK version pinning strategy
body:
## Issue Description
Inconsistent SDK version pinning leading to potential build issues.

## Current State
- Workspace: 21.7.7
- Some contracts: hardcoded versions
- No patch version strategy

## Recommended Strategy
```toml
[workspace.dependencies]
soroban-sdk = "=21.7.7"  # Exact version
```

## Update Process
1. Test new SDK version
2. Update workspace version
3. Run full test suite
4. Update all contracts
5. Document changes

## Automation
- Dependabot for SDK updates
- Automated testing
- Version compatibility matrix

title: Add missing contract verification for block explorers

Contracts not verified on Stellar explorers, reducing transparency.

title: Implement proper Soroban auth pattern for all functions
Add comprehensive Soroban contract template
body:
## Issue Description
No standardized template for new contract development.

## Template Structure
```
new_contract/
├── Cargo.toml
├── src/
│   ├── lib.rs
│   ├── types.rs
│   ├── errors.rs
│   ├── events.rs
│   ├── test.rs
│   └── utils.rs
└── tests/
    └── integration.rs
```

## Template Features
- Standard initialization
- Access control boilerplate
- Error definitions
- Event emissions
- Test scaffolding
- CI configuration

## Generation
```bash
make new-contract NAME=my_contract
```

## Benefits
- Consistency
- Best practices inclusion
- Faster development
- Reduced errors

title: Implement automated Soroban contract size optimization
body:
## Issue Description
No automated WASM size optimization in build process.

## Optimization Techniques
- Dead code elimination
- Function inlining control
- Dependency minimization
- Compression optimization

## Build Configuration
```toml
[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"        # Optimize for size
lto = true             # Link-time optimization
codegen-units = 1      # Single codegen unit
strip = "symbols"      # Remove symbols
```

## CI Checks
- Warn at 50KB
- Error at 60KB
- Optimization suggestions
- Size trend tracking

## Tools
- wasm-opt
- twiggy (profiling)
- wasm-size
- Custom scripts

title: Add comprehensive contract security checklist
body:
## Issue Description
No standardized security checklist for contract development.

## Checklist Items
### Access Control
- [ ] Admin functions protected
- [ ] Role-based access implemented
- [ ] Authorization checks present

### Input Validation
- [ ] All inputs validated
- [ ] Length limits enforced
- [ ] Format validation complete

### Arithmetic Safety
- [ ] Checked arithmetic used
- [ ] Overflow protection
- [ ] Division by zero prevented

### State Management
- [ ] Consistent state updates
- [ ] Reentrancy protection
- [ ] Proper initialization

### Events
- [ ] Critical actions logged
- [ ] Events indexed properly
- [ ] Complete audit trail

## Integration
- PR template requirement
- Automated checks
- Audit requirement

title: Fix missing Soroban storage tier optimization
body:
## Issue Description
Not utilizing Soroban's storage tiers (persistent, temporary, instance) optimally.

## Storage Tier Guidelines
- **Persistent**: Critical data, long-lived
- **Temporary**: Session data, short-lived
- **Instance**: Contract metadata, config

## Optimization
```rust
// Good: Appropriate tier usage
env.storage().persistent().set(&DataKey::Balance, &balance);
env.storage().temporary().set(&DataKey::Session(token), &session);
env.storage().instance().set(&DataKey::Admin, &admin);
```

## Common Mistakes
- Using persistent for temporary data
- Not setting TTL for temporary storage
- Instance storage for per-user data

## Cost Savings
- 50-70% reduction with proper tiers
- Better performance
- Efficient resource usage

## Audit
- Review all storage usage
- Migrate to appropriate tiers
- Add TTL management

title: Implement comprehensive incident response plan
body:
## Issue Description
No documented incident response plan for contract vulnerabilities.

## Incident Types
1. Smart contract bug
2. Exploit detected
3. Data breach
4. Service disruption
5. Governance attack

## Response Procedures
### Immediate (0-1 hour)
- Pause affected contracts
- Assess impact scope
- Notify team

### Short-term (1-24 hours)
- Deploy fix if available
- Communicate with users
- Engage security auditors

### Long-term (1-7 days)
- Root cause analysis
- Implement permanent fix
- Update security measures
- Public disclosure

## Communication
- Status page updates
- Community notifications
- Regulatory reporting
- Post-incident report

title: Implement comprehensive smart contract audit preparation
body:
## Issue Description
No systematic preparation process for external security audits.

## Pre-Audit Checklist
### Code Quality
- [ ] All tests passing
- [ ] No compiler warnings
- [ ] Documentation complete
- [ ] Code comments adequate

### Security
- [ ] Internal review completed
- [ ] Static analysis clean
- [ ] Known issues documented
- [ ] Test coverage > 80%

### Documentation
- [ ] Architecture diagrams
- [ ] Threat model
- [ ] Specification docs
- [ ] Known limitations

## Audit Scope
- Critical contracts first
- Payment flows
- Access control
- Cross-chain bridges

## Post-Audit
- Fix critical issues within 48 hours
- Address high within 1 week
- Publish audit report
- Implement recommendations

title: Add comprehensive Soroban CLI usage documentation
body:
## Issue Description
Insufficient documentation for Soroban CLI operations.

## Required Guides
### Deployment
- Local network setup
- Testnet deployment
- Mainnet deployment
- Contract initialization

### Interaction
- Function invocation
- Event monitoring
- State inspection
- Transaction building

### Development
- Testing commands
- Build optimization
- Debug techniques
- Performance profiling

## Examples
```bash
# Deploy contract
soroban contract deploy \
    --wasm ./contract.wasm \
    --source alice \
    --network testnet

# Invoke function
soroban contract invoke \
    --id CONTRACT_ID \
    --function transfer \
    --arg-to RECIPIENT \
    --arg-amount 1000
```

## Format
- Step-by-step tutorials
- Troubleshooting section
- Common patterns
- Best practices

title: Implement proper contract versioning and release process
body:
## Issue Description
No formal versioning and release process for contracts.

## Versioning Strategy
- Semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH)
- Breaking changes = MAJOR
- New features = MINOR
- Bug fixes = PATCH

## Release Process
1. Version bump
2. Changelog update
3. Tag creation
4. Build verification
5. Deployment
6. Announcement

## Automation
```bash
make release VERSION=1.2.0
```

## Changelog Format
```markdown
## [1.2.0] - 2026-04-20
### Added
- New feature X
### Fixed
- Bug Y
### Changed
- Improved Z
```

## Requirements
- Automated releases
- Release notes generation
- Artifact publication
- Notification system

title: Add comprehensive Soroban network configuration management
body:
## Issue Description
Manual network configuration prone to errors across environments.

## Configuration Management
```toml
[networks.local]
rpc-url = "http://localhost:8000"
network-passphrase = "Standalone Network ; February 2017"

[networks.testnet]
rpc-url = "https://soroban-testnet.stellar.org"
network-passphrase = "Test SDF Network ; September 2015"

[networks.mainnet]
rpc-url = "https://soroban-mainnet.stellar.org"
network-passphrase = "Public Global Stellar Network ; September 2015"
```

## Features
- Environment-specific configs
- Validation checks
- Auto-detection
- Fallback mechanisms

## Safety
- Mainnet confirmation prompts
- Transaction simulation
- Dry-run mode
- Network verification

title: Fix potential Soroban serialization edge cases
body:
## Issue Description
Potential serialization failures with edge case data structures.

## Edge Cases
- Empty collections
- Nested structures depth
- Large data payloads
- Circular references
- Null values

## Testing Required
```rust
#[test]
fn test_serialization_edge_cases() {
    // Empty vec
    let empty_vec = Vec::<u64>::new(&env);
    assert!(serialize(&empty_vec).is_ok());
    
    // Deep nesting
    // Large maps
    // Maximum size strings
}
```

## Validation
- Size limits enforcement
- Depth restrictions
- Cycle detection
- Memory limits

## Impact
- Prevent runtime panics
- Ensure data integrity
- Avoid storage corruption

title: Implement comprehensive Soroban event replay system
body:

No mechanism to replay events for recovery or analytics.

- Event storage

```rust
pub struct EventReplay {
    pub from_ledger: u32,
    pub to_ledger: Option<u32>,
    pub event_types: Vec<Symbol>,
    pub addresses: Vec<Address>,
}

pub fn replay_events(env: Env, config: EventReplay) -> Result<Vec<Event>, Error> {
    // Retrieve and filter events
}
```

## Use Cases
- State recovery
- Audit reconstruction
- Analytics generation
- Debug assistance

## Performance
- Batch retrieval
- Efficient filtering
- Pagination support
- Caching strategy

title: Add missing Soroban contract metadata standards
body:

Contracts lack standardized metadata for discovery and integration.

## Metadata Schema
```json

  "name": "Contract Name",

  "license": "MIT",

```

- Better documentation
- Easier integration
- Discovery mechanisms

- Update on deployment
